ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general naan bread entry (FOODON_03302051); Tara Naan is a specific variant with semolina and almond flakes not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for naan or Tara Naan; A000E (Bread and similar products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Tara Naan or naan bread variant with almond topping.

preparation_method: made with semolina and refined flour
leavened with yeast or baking powder

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Tara Naan weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served at an Indian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Naan bread, plain — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for naan bread (FDC ID 325871) for micronutrient detail
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Tara Naan is a restaurant-specific variant of plain naan enriched with semolina and topped with almond flakes. Macronutrient base is adapted from ICMR-NIN plain naan values. Almond topping adds modest fat and protein; semolina substitution slightly increases protein and reduces glycemic response compared to pure maida naan. Values represent a best-estimate composite. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors (4/4/9 kcal per g protein/carbohydrate/fat).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Diabetes Care 2021; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for naan bread (GI ~57, white wheat flour base)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tara Naan specifically. GI of 57 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain naan bread made with refined wheat flour (GI ~57, medium). The semolina component of Tara Naan may modestly lower the effective GI relative to pure maida naan; however, in the absence of a direct measurement, GI 57 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 63.0) / 100 ≈ 36.0 for a 120 g serving (carbohydrate ~63 g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~456 mg per standard 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Tara Naan to one piece per meal and avoid pairing with other high-sodium dishes such as pickles or salty curries, as the combined sodium load can exceed recommended single-meal limits of 600–800 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (~36) driven by refined flour and semolina base with moderate GI of 57
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Tara Naan as a high-glycemic-load food; portion size should be restricted to half a piece (approximately 60 g) and paired with high-fibre vegetables or legume-based curries to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate content and moderate saturated fat from milk and ghee; almond topping provides beneficial monounsaturated fats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, Tara Naan should be consumed in moderation; the almond topping contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fats, but the refined flour base and moderate sodium content warrant limiting intake to one piece and choosing lower-fat curry accompaniments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tara Naan is a leavened Indian flatbread made from a combination of refined wheat flour (maida) and semolina (sooji), enriched with milk for softness and topped with almond flakes before baking. It is traditionally cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) or a conventional oven, producing a bread with a slightly crisp exterior, a soft and chewy interior, and a subtly nutty flavour from the almond topping. The semolina component gives the bread a slightly coarser texture and a mild golden colour compared to a standard plain naan. Tara Naan is served as an accompaniment to Indian curries, dals, and grilled dishes, and is popular in Indian restaurants in Singapore, including at establishments such as Indian House/Dumpukht Grills & Curry. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy, carbohydrate-dominant food (~297 kcal per 100 g, ~52.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g) with a medium glycemic index of approximately 57 and a high glycemic load per standard serving (~36), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. The almond topping contributes beneficial monounsaturated fats and a small amount of additional protein. Sodium content is moderate (~380 mg per 100 g) and should be considered in the context of the full meal for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Contains gluten, dairy, and tree nuts (almonds); not suitable for individuals with these allergies.

What is the difference between Tara Naan and plain naan?
Is Tara Naan suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Tara Naan is a restaurant-specific variant of naan bread not directly listed in ICMR-NIN or USDA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain naan data with adjustments for semolina and almond flake content. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (plain naan, GI ~57) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because no direct laboratory analysis of this specific product is available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on ICMR-NIN plain naan and USDA SR Legacy naan data, adjusted for semolina and almond flake additions; direct laboratory analysis of Tara Naan as served at Indian House/Dumpukht Grills & Curry is not available. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN naan entry or the USDA SR Legacy naan entry used as the primary source. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source; naan is not a significant source of B12. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source for plain naan. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source; almond topping would contribute a small amount but no reliable per-100g figure is available for this composite. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary ICMR-NIN source. 9. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in baked leavened flatbreads made without partially hydrogenated oils per USDA SR Legacy data. 10. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — naan bread contains no vitamin C-contributing ingredients. 11. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain naan, refined wheat flour base); no direct GI measurement for Tara Naan exists in the literature. 12. Serving size of 120 g is an estimate for one standard restaurant-served piece of naan; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 13. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules for Indian cuisine served in Singapore.
